commands.description: Initialize sfdt configuration for a Salesforce DX project
Deploy to a Salesforce org (interactive manifest flow, or --smart delta deploy)

options.name: dryRun
options.description: Show what would be executed (smart mode: validate-only)

options.name: sourceDir
options.description: Deploy a source directory instead of a manifest (relative to project root)

options.name: smart
options.description: Smart delta deploy: only changed metadata, with smart test selection

options.name: deltaBase
options.description: Base git ref for the smart delta (default: config or "main")

options.name: deltaHead
options.description: Head git ref for the smart delta

options.name: prod
options.description: Treat the target org as production (never downgrade tests)

options.name: aiDeps
options.description: Run AI dependency cleanup on the computed delta before deploying

options.name: aiFix
options.description: On failure, run AI deploy-error analysis, or the bounded auto-fix loop when ai.agent is enabled (CLI providers only)

options.name: prComment
options.description: Post the smart-deploy delta + outcome to the current PR (via gh)

options.name: agent
options.description: Non-interactive agent mode (no AI prompts block on input)

options.name: tag
options.description: Tag the release in git (v<version>) after a successful deploy (standard manifest deploy only)

options.name: createPr
options.description: Create a PR from the current branch to the default branch (via gh) after a successful deploy (standard manifest deploy only)

options.name: notify
options.description: Send the deploy success/failure notification through the configured channels (smart and standard manifest deploys)

commands.description: Generate a release manifest and optionally AI-powered release notes

options.name: package
options.description: Package directory to generate manifest for: a short name or "all"

commands.description: Run Apex tests with the enhanced test runner (or --logic for unified Apex + Flow tests)

options.name: dryRun
options.description: Show what would be executed without running

options.name: classNames
options.description: Run only these Apex test classes (comma-separated); overrides the configured test classes for this run

options.name: logic
options.description: Run Apex + Flow tests together via `sf logic run test` (Spring '26 beta; needs "View All Data")

options.name: lwc
options.description: Run the project's LWC (Jest) unit tests locally (npm run test:unit or sfdx-lwc-jest)

options.name: tests
options.description: For --logic: comma-separated test names (Apex classes and FlowTesting.<name>)

options.name: category
options.description: For --logic: restrict to Apex or Flow

options.name: wait
options.description: For --logic: streaming wait timeout in whole minutes, >= 1 (default: 30)

options.name: allowZeroTests
options.description: For --logic: exit 0 even when Salesforce runs zero tests (default: zero tests fail the run)

commands.description: Run an Agentforce agent test as a CI gate (via `sf agent test run`), with pass/fail exit code, optional notification, and PR decoration

options.name: spec
options.description: Agent test API name (AiEvaluationDefinition) to run

options.name: threshold
options.description: Pass if the aggregate pass rate is >= this percent (0-100), rather than requiring every test to pass

options.name: notify
options.description: Dispatch an agent-test-success/failure notification via configured channels

options.name: prComment
options.description: Post the pass/fail result to the current PR (via gh)

commands.description: Pull metadata changes from the default org

options.name: dryRun
options.description: show what would be retrieved without retrieving

options.name: full
options.description: force full retrieve and rebuild cache

commands.description: Run code quality analysis and optionally generate an AI fix plan

options.name: fixPlan
options.description: Generate an AI-powered fix plan from quality output

options.name: generateStubs
options.description: Generate @IsTest stub classes for untested Apex classes

options.name: includeFixes
options.description: Ask Code Analyzer v5 for actionable fixes/suggestions in the scan output (feeds the AI fix plan)

options.name: outputFile
options.description: Also write the Code Analyzer v5 results to a file; the format follows the extension (e.g. .sarif for code-scanning upload)

options.name: allowLegacyAnalyzer
options.description: Permit the legacy Code Analyzer v4 (sf scanner run) when v5 is unavailable — results are non-authoritative (also: config quality.analyzer.allowLegacyV4)

options.name: agent
options.description: Non-interactive agent mode (do not block waiting on the AI fix-plan session)

options.name: api67
options.description: Run only the API v67 (Summer '26) user-mode readiness scan of local Apex sources

options.name: testHints
options.description: Run only the RunRelevantTests hint check: flag @IsTest classes with no @IsTest(testFor=...) annotation (Spring '26)

options.name: json
options.description: Emit structured JSON to stdout (only honoured with --api67 / --test-hints)

commands.description: Run pre-deployment validation checks

options.name: dryRun
options.description: Show what would be executed without running

commands.description: Roll back a deployment to a target org

options.name: dryRun
options.description: Show what would be executed without running

options.name: json
options.description: Emit structured JSON to stdout (CI mode)

commands.description: Run post-deployment smoke tests against a target org

options.name: org
options.description: Target org alias for smoke tests

options.name: dryRun
options.description: Show what would be executed without running

commands.description: AI-powered Salesforce code review of current branch changes

options.name: agent
options.description: Non-interactive agent mode (do not block waiting on the AI session)

commands.description: Send a notification (events: deploy-success, deploy-failure, test-failure, release-created, snapshot)
Detect metadata drift between local source and a target org

options.name: org
options.description: Target org alias to check for drift

options.name: json
options.description: Emit structured JSON to stdout (CI mode)

commands.description: Manage project CHANGELOG

subcommands.description: Use AI to generate [Unreleased] entries from git history

options.name: package
options.description: Scope changelog to a specific package directory

subcommands.description: Move [Unreleased] changes to a new version section
Verify [Unreleased] content against git changes

commands.description: Smart package.xml generator from git diffs (with optional AI dependency cleanup)

options.name: base
options.description: Base git ref to diff from

options.name: head
options.description: Head git ref to diff to

options.name: destructive
options.description: Write destructive changes to this path (otherwise skipped)

options.name: aiCleanup
options.description: Run AI dependency analysis on the generated manifest

options.name: aiCleanup
options.description: Skip AI dependency analysis even when AI is enabled

options.name: print
options.description: Print the generated package.xml to stdout instead of writing a file

options.name: package
options.description: Package directory to diff: a short name matching packageDirectories or "all"

options.name: name
options.description: Release label for output filename (semver, free-form, or "today")

commands.description: AI-powered analysis of a Salesforce deployment error log

options.name: fromStdin
options.description: Read the deployment log from stdin

options.name: latest
options.description: Use the most recent log in the configured log directory (default)

options.name: agent
options.description: Non-interactive agent mode (do not block waiting on the AI session)

commands.description: Generate a PR description or Slack message from deployment changes

options.name: output
options.description: Write result to this file instead of stdout

options.name: commitLimit
options.description: Max commits to include in the context sent to AI

commands.description: Launch the local SFDT web dashboard (test results, drift, preflight)

options.name: open
options.description: Do not automatically open the browser

commands.description: Compare metadata between two orgs or an org and local source

options.name: output
options.description: Write a package.xml of source-only items to this file

commands.description: Output shell completion script (bash, zsh, or fish)
Update sfdt to the latest version from npm
Read and write .sfdt config values

subcommands.description: Set a config value using dot notation (e.g. deployment.coverageThreshold)
Print a config value using dot notation (e.g. defaultOrg)

commands.description: AI utilities

subcommands.description: Run a prompt through the configured AI provider and print the result

commands.description: Fetch complete metadata inventory from an org

options.name: output
options.description: Write JSON to this path (ignored if --json is passed) (default: logs/scan-latest.json)

options.name: format
options.description: Output format: json | table (ignored if --json is passed) (default: json)

options.name: json
options.description: Emit structured JSON to stdout and exit without writing a file

commands.description: Show metadata dependencies for a component (references + referenced-by)

options.name: type
options.description: Metadata type to resolve (ApexClass, ApexTrigger, ApexPage, ApexComponent, Flow, LightningComponentBundle, AuraDefinitionBundle, CustomField)

options.name: gaps
options.description: Report source-parsed references the Tooling API may miss (offline; pass --org to diff)

commands.description: Report Apex code coverage (org-wide + per-class)

options.name: threshold
options.description: Fail (exit non-zero) if org-wide coverage is below this percent

commands.description: Diagnose org health — audit trail, licenses, MFA coverage & enforcement readiness, SOAP login() retirement, unused/unreferenced Apex, inactive users/flows/validations/workflows, API versions, permission sets, connected apps, field descriptions, object & field access

subcommands.description: Run every audit check and write a snapshot

options.name: notify
options.description: Send the snapshot to configured notification channels

subcommands.description: Run only the "audittrail" audit check
Run only the "licenses" audit check
Run only the "mfa" audit check
Run only the "mfa-readiness" audit check
Run only the "soap-logins" audit check
Run only the "unused-apex" audit check
Run only the "inactive-users" audit check
Run only the "api-versions" audit check
Run only the "inactive-flows" audit check
Run only the "unused-permsets" audit check
Run only the "connected-apps" audit check
Run only the "field-descriptions" audit check
Run only the "apex-unreferenced" audit check
Run only the "lint-access" audit check
Run only the "inactive-validations" audit check
Run only the "inactive-workflows" audit check
Run only the "lint-access-fields" audit check

commands.description: Monitor org health — limits (incl. elastic async Apex), Apex failures, security score, org info & release version, deployment history, legacy API usage, paused flows, and metadata backup

subcommands.description: Run every monitoring check (add --backup to include a metadata backup)

options.name: backup
options.description: Also run a full metadata backup

options.name: notify
options.description: Send the snapshot to configured notification channels

subcommands.description: Run only the "limits" monitoring check
Run only the "errors" monitoring check
Run only the "health" monitoring check
Run only the "org-info" monitoring check
Run only the "deploy-history" monitoring check
Run only the "deprecated-api" monitoring check
Run only the "flow-errors" monitoring check
Retrieve a full metadata backup into the configured backup directory
Generate a scheduled org-monitoring CI workflow (alias for "ci init --type monitor")

options.name: provider
options.description: CI provider: github | gitlab | azure | bitbucket

options.name: auth
options.description: Org authentication: sfdx-url | jwt (default: config ci.authMethod or sfdx-url)

options.name: node
options.description: Node.js version for the CI runner (@sfdt/cli requires >= 22.15)

options.name: out
options.description: Output file path (defaults to the provider convention)

options.name: print
options.description: Print to stdout instead of writing a file

commands.description: Generate project documentation (objects, Apex, flows) with optional AI overview and diagrams

subcommands.description: Generate MkDocs-compatible markdown documentation for the project

options.name: ai
options.description: Enrich the index with an AI-written project overview (default: on when config features.ai and docs.ai allow)

options.name: ai
options.description: Skip the AI overview even when enabled in config

options.name: roles
options.description: Also generate per-component Developer/Admin/User/DevOps guides (AI); optional comma list to subset, e.g. --roles developer,admin (default: config docs.roleGuides + docs.roles)

options.name: diagrams
options.description: Skip the standalone ER-diagram page even when docs.diagrams is enabled in config

subcommands.description: Print (or write) a Mermaid ER diagram of the data model

options.name: output
options.description: Write the diagram to this path instead of stdout

commands.description: Import/export org data sets (native sf tree commands) for sandbox & scratch seeding

subcommands.description: List configured data sets
Export records for a data set from an org (sf data export tree)
Import a previously-exported data set into an org (sf data import tree)
Bulk-delete the records targeted by a data set

options.name: yes
options.description: Skip the confirmation prompt (required for non-interactive use)

commands.description: Create, delete, list, and pool Salesforce scratch orgs

subcommands.description: Create a scratch org from the configured definition file

options.name: alias
options.description: Alias for the new scratch org

subcommands.description: Delete a scratch org by alias or username

options.name: yes
options.description: Skip the confirmation prompt (required for non-interactive use)

subcommands.description: List active scratch orgs
Manage a pool of pre-created scratch orgs
Show the current scratch org pool state
Create scratch orgs until the pool reaches the configured size

commands.description: Flow-specific analyses (CLI uses @sfdt/flow-core, matching the Chrome extension)

subcommands.description: Run flow-core health analysis on every Flow with an active version

options.name: output
options.description: Write report to this path (default: logs/flow-scan-latest.json)

options.name: json
options.description: Emit the report to stdout instead of writing a file

subcommands.description: List record-triggered Flow groups that fire on the same object + timing + event

options.name: output
options.description: Write report to this path (default: logs/flow-conflicts-latest.json)

options.name: json
options.description: Emit the report to stdout instead of writing a file

commands.description: Manage the Chrome extension bridge (native messaging host)

subcommands.description: Install the Chrome native messaging host manifest for the SFDT SF Helper extension

options.name: extensionId
options.description: The Chrome extension ID (32 lowercase letters a–p; find it at chrome://extensions with Developer Mode on)

options.name: browser
options.description: Browser to register with — one of chrome, edge, brave, chromium, vivaldi, all (default: chrome)

options.name: projectRoot
options.description: Salesforce project root to record for the host (default: the current project). The host reads this to serve the read-only kinds (drift/scan/compare/org-health).

subcommands.description: Remove the native messaging host manifest from one or all browsers

options.name: browser
options.description: Browser to remove — one of chrome, edge, brave, chromium, vivaldi, all (default: all)

subcommands.description: Show the latest telemetry snapshot the extension pushed to .sfdt/telemetry-snapshot.json

options.name: limit
options.description: Cap the number of features shown (default: 10)

subcommands.description: Report which browsers have the native host manifest installed

commands.description: Manage the extension kill-switch (.sfdt/feature-flags.json). Disabled features are skipped at extension boot.

subcommands.description: List currently disabled feature ids
Add a feature id to the disabled list (creates the file if missing)
Remove a feature id from the disabled list
Re-enable everything (empties the disabled list; leaves the file in place)

options.name: remove
options.description: Delete the file entirely instead of writing { disabled: [] }

commands.description: Diagnose the local sfdt environment and extension stack

options.name: core
options.description: Run only the environment checks (sf, node, git, config, AI, org)

options.name: extension
options.description: Run only the extension-stack checks (bridge, native host, feature flags, telemetry)

options.name: org
options.description: Org alias for the connectivity check (default: config defaultOrg)

options.name: port
options.description: Localhost port the bridge listens on

commands.description: Manage the Model Context Protocol (MCP) server

subcommands.description: Start the sfdt MCP server in stdio mode
Purge expired parked results from the cache directory

commands.description: Manage sfdt CLI plugins

subcommands.description: Scaffold a new sfdt CLI plugin project

commands.description: Manage sfdt agent skills

subcommands.description: Export local agent skills to IDE/agent-specific configurations

options.name: target
options.description: Target IDE/Agent format (claude | cursor | codex | windsurf | pack)

commands.description: Generate CI/CD pipeline templates (monitoring, smart deploy, release, scratch-org CI)

subcommands.description: Generate a ready-to-use CI workflow for a provider

options.name: provider
options.description: CI provider: github | gitlab | azure | bitbucket

options.name: type
options.description: Workflow type: monitor | deploy | release | scratch

options.name: auth
options.description: Org authentication: sfdx-url | jwt (default: config ci.authMethod or sfdx-url)

options.name: runner
options.description: CLI runner: npx | docker | action (docker = official sfdt image, gitlab/bitbucket; action = the scoobydrew83/sfdt@v0 composite action, github)

options.name: org
options.description: Target org alias (Dev Hub alias for --type scratch; defaults to config.defaultOrg)

options.name: branch
options.description: Protected branch that triggers a release workflow (defaults to config.defaultBranch or main)

options.name: environment
options.description: Approval environment for a release workflow (default: config ci.environment or production)

options.name: deltaBase
options.description: Base git ref for smart-deploy delta (release: fallback when no tag exists; default HEAD~1)

options.name: definitionFile
options.description: Scratch org definition file for --type scratch (defaults to config scratch.definitionFile)

options.name: node
options.description: Node.js version for the CI runner (@sfdt/cli requires >= 22.15)

options.name: out
options.description: Output file path (defaults to the provider convention)

options.name: print
options.description: Print to stdout instead of writing a file

commands.description: Pull-request helpers (post deploy/monitor/audit results as comments)

subcommands.description: Post a comment to the current PR (snapshot summary, a file, or inline text)

options.name: type
options.description: Render the latest snapshot: audit | monitor

options.name: body
options.description: Post this inline text instead of a snapshot

options.name: file
options.description: Post the contents of this file (relative to project root)

options.name: pr
options.description: Target PR number or URL (defaults to the current branch PR)

commands.sideEffects: posts a comment on the current pull request

commands.description: Retrofit metadata from a source org: retrieve → commit → smart-deploy to a target org

options.name: target
options.description: Org to deploy changes TO (omit with --no-deploy)

options.name: metadata
options.description: Comma-separated metadata types to retrieve (defaults to a common admin-changed set)

options.name: commitMsg
options.description: Commit message for the retrofit commit

options.name: commit
options.description: Retrieve only; leave changes uncommitted for review

options.name: deploy
options.description: Retrieve and commit, but do not deploy to a target

options.name: execute
options.description: Perform a real deploy to the target (default is validate-only)

commands.description: Show recent sfdt run history (audit, monitor, quality, test, deploy, agent-test, …) from the local run index

options.name: type
options.description: Filter to one run type (e.g. audit | monitor | quality | test-run | deploy | agent-test)

commands.description: Audit Salesforce API versions across local source and the org (Apex, Flow, LWC, Aura vs the org max)

options.name: localOnly
options.description: Skip the org side even when an org is configured

options.name: advise
options.description: AI upgrade advice for local components below the target version (requires features.ai; grounded by the curated per-version registry)

options.name: target
options.description: For --advise: target API version (default: the org max, or the registry max offline)

options.name: type
options.description: For --advise: restrict to one family — apex | flow | lwc

options.name: json
options.description: Emit the full report as JSON

commands.description: Print the sfdt version
